action: Collect symbol lookup results, endpoint response samples, or schema differences.
done_when: Original evidence is saved with date/time and source context.
public_user_value: Users can report what they actually saw without needing to code.

action: Convert the observation into a consistent template with requested symbols, returned symbols, endpoint, and result state.
done_when: Required fields are populated or marked unknown.
public_user_value: Different reports can be compared side by side.

action: Apply result interpretation rules such as success, partial result, missing requested symbol, null result, or schema drift.
done_when: Reasoning is recorded in plain language.
public_user_value: Users can tell whether an item is a Yahoo behavior, tool limitation, or unknown.

action: Repeat the observation when practical using the same symbol set or endpoint.
done_when: At least one retest or reason for no retest is documented.
public_user_value: Reduces false alarms from temporary rate limits or tool quirks.

action: Promote repeated or well-supported observations into confirmed records.
public_user_value: Confirmed records can support release notes.

action: Update CSV tables and workbook sheets only when the change affects fields, schemas, symbols, states, or interpretation rules.
done_when: Data remains sorted, validated, and versioned.
public_user_value: Reference files stay useful rather than becoming a discussion log.

action: Update README, CHANGELOG, MANIFEST, and release notes.
done_when: Public users can understand what changed.
public_user_value: Users can upgrade without reading every file.

action: Create a normal commit-ready ZIP with repository folders intact.
done_when: ZIP opens with 7-Zip and includes manifest.
public_user_value: One package can be uploaded to GitHub or inspected locally.

action: Commit to GitHub and optionally create a GitHub Release.
done_when: Release tag, title, and assets match version.
public_user_value: Public users can download a specific version.
